title: Antibiotic Disk Testing
author: Aubrey Reed
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ugfettn

steps.instruction: Fill Eppendorf tube with 1mL of media (LB or TSA) Using sterile loop pick up one colony from the plate and suspend in media tube (being sure to match medias, LB plate -> LB liquid media) Leave to grow overnight (Or for 2 or 3 days)

Take 10uL from the overnight cultures and plate and spread on plate Place antibiotic to be tested on plate as pictured below Check for growth and inhibition after 24 hours Measure the diameter of inhibition and compare to known measures of inhibition to determine susceptibility.
